note: Non-regression budgets for stable catalog artifacts. A candidate artifact must not regress any metric beyond its budget relative to the baseline. Budgets are relative (factor or absolute delta). PR 4 of #21 defines initial budgets; they may be tightened but not loosened without an issue.

budgets.note: ONNX vs PyTorch reference. Must not exceed threshold.
Real-time factor. Must not exceed baseline * factor.
Output shape must match expected. No budget — must always be true.
No NaN allowed. No budget — must always be false.
No Inf allowed. No budget — must always be false.

promotion_policy.note: From issue #21: until empirical baselines are frozen, the initial promotion policy applies. After freezing, a candidate must not regress any metric beyond its budget.
initial_policy: Structural graph rewrites must remain within the existing numerical tolerance and show no quality regression.
A new default model must be non-inferior to the current default on leakage and aggregate SI-SDR.
A target-specific artifact must improve at least one of installed size, cold load, warm latency, peak memory, or quality without regressing another metric outside its budget.
A precision/quantization variant that is slower or fails quality limits is not published, and its shipping code is deleted.
promotion_policy.pareto_requirement: Every new format, precision, provider, or student model must include a Pareto comparison against the current default. A candidate that is dominated by the default on all measured metrics is not published.
promotion_policy.no_bypass: Maintainers cannot bypass a failed gate by editing release notes. The gate is enforced by the catalog publish workflow.
